title: Basic
summary: Defines ordered compositions I=(I_1,...,I_r) of an integer N.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines ordered compositions I=(I_1,...,I_r) of an integer N.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Juhlformula/Juhloperators/Basic.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: An ordered composition of N. Mathematically this is a finite ordered tuple I = (I_1,..., I_r) with positive entries and total sum N. Its mathematical data are: parts: List Nat; positive: for every n, n belongs to parts maps to 0 < n; sum eq: parts.sum equals N.
The length of a composition. In explicit terms, for i of type composition n, its defining expression is I.parts.length.
The empty composition of 0. In explicit terms, its defining expression is parts : equals positive : equals by intro n hn cases hn sum eq : equals by simp.

The singleton composition (N), for N > 0. In explicit terms, for n in the natural numbers, and hn of type 0 < n, its defining expression is parts : equals N positive : equals by intro n hn simp at hn subst n exact h N sum eq : equals by simp.
correspondence.leanType: def singleton (N : Nat) (hN : 0 < N) : Composition N

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For N in the natural numbers, and hN of type 0 < N, singleton N h N.parts equals N.
correspondence.leanType: theorem singleton_parts (N : Nat) (hN : 0 < N) : (singleton N hN).parts = [N]

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For N in the natural numbers, and hN of type 0 < N, singleton N h N.length equals 1.
correspondence.leanType: theorem singleton_length (N : Nat) (hN : 0 < N) : (singleton N hN).length = 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The first nontrivial two-part composition (a,b). In explicit terms, for a, b in the natural numbers, ha of type 0 < a, and hb of type 0 < b, its defining expression is parts : equals a, b positive : equals by intro n hn simp at hn rcases hn with rfl | rfl · exact ha · exact hb sum eq : equals by simp.
correspondence.leanType: def pair (a b : Nat) (ha : 0 < a) (hb : 0 < b) : Composition (a + b)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For a, b in the natural numbers, ha of type 0 < a, and hb of type 0 < b, pair a b ha hb.parts equals a, b.
correspondence.leanType: theorem pair_parts (a b : Nat) (ha : 0 < a) (hb : 0 < b) : (pair a b ha hb).parts = [a, b]

references.note: Source for ordered GJMS compositions and coefficients.

references.locator: Direct ambient proof of Juhl's formulas
references.note: Ambient interpretation of the composition formula.

limitations: Verified means that Lean checks the propositions encoded in this file and their imported assumptions. It does not assert that the present formal statement exhausts every analytic hypothesis or convention in the cited literature.
